ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of media and entertainment, Netflix Inc. continues to adapt to shifting consumer preferences,
particularly as it pertains to social media engagement and gaming revenue across various platforms. The trends in Daily Active
Users (DAU) and Monthly Active Users (MAU) reveal significant fluctuations, notably with Q2's DAU peaking at 50M, while
Q3 demonstrated a remarkable increase to 182M. These figures underscore the growing importance of user engagement metrics,
which are critical for assessing content popularity and platform loyalty. Furthermore, as the gaming sector expands, it is
essential to analyze revenue streams by platform.
Year  DAU
MonthlyActiveUsers  TotalRevenue
2020  2021  2022  GrowthRate
Q1  Q2  Q3  Q4  AverageRevenue  ChurnRate
[HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  $25B  15%  30M
For instance, the total revenue from Console gaming in 2021 reached $1.3B, while PC gaming generated $3.8B in 2020 and is
projected to rise to $4.5B by 2022. The gaming revenue landscape reflects broader trends in the industry, with the Streaming
segment showcasing a robust performance, contributing significantly to the overall financial health of the company.
Platform  Revenue(USD)  NetIncome(USD)
Console  $5.2B  $5.5B  $6.0B  $1.2B  [HIDDEN]  $1.4B
PC  [HIDDEN]  $4.1B  [HIDDEN]  $800M  $900M  $950M
Notably, the average revenue per user (ARPU) continues to be a focal point of analysis, as it directly correlates with churn rates
and user retention strategies. These metrics collectively provide insights into Netflix’s strategic positioning and operational
effectiveness within the competitive media and entertainment sector.
